scope: TDD thin VS Code and Obsidian editor clients against the Rust broker /v1/autocomplete protocol.

summary.result: Minimal editor client artifacts are implemented and tested. VS Code and Obsidian clients share generated protocol constants/schemas plus one broker client/session helper, construct the same AutocompleteRequest shape, debounce calls, abort and cancel stale request_ids, and do not import provider-pi internals.

tdd_cycles.behavior: Shared client artifact constructs protocol-v1 autocomplete requests and sends POST /v1/autocomplete.
Debounced broker session cancels stale in-flight request_ids and ignores late stale responses.
VS Code host glue collects document/cursor context and renders fresh broker responses as inline completion ghost text.
tdd_cycles.implementation: clients/vscode/src/extension-core.js and extension registration in clients/vscode/src/extension.js.

tdd_cycles.behavior: Obsidian host glue collects note context, cancels stale requests, renders CodeMirror ghost text, and accepts with Tab.
tdd_cycles.implementation: clients/obsidian/src/plugin-core.js and CodeMirror ViewPlugin/keymap in clients/obsidian/src/main.js.

tdd_cycles.behavior: Editor clients stay provider-agnostic and do not reference provider-pi internals.
tdd_cycles.implementation: Clients import only protocol/shared broker client helpers.

commands_run.result: passed; generated protocol constants and request/response JSON Schemas from the Rust protocol crate.

passed; 9 Node tests cover protocol shape, endpoint use, stale cancellation, VS Code request construction/provider behavior, Obsidian request construction/controller behavior, and provider-boundary scanning.

commands_run.command: node --check clients/obsidian/src/main.js && node --check clients/vscode/src/extension.js && node --check clients/protocol/src/client.js
commands_run.result: passed; host glue files parse without requiring editor runtime modules.
passed; existing Rust workspace tests still pass after adding the protocol artifact export binary.

success_criteria.same_protocol: passed: both clients use shared clients/protocol BrokerClient and generated /v1/autocomplete constants.
success_criteria.settings: passed: VS Code contributes brokerUrl/debounceMs/maxContextChars settings; Obsidian has matching plugin settings with a minimal setting tab.
success_criteria.stale_cancellation: passed: shared session aborts local fetches and calls /v1/cancel/:request_id; VS Code and Obsidian tests cover stale request replacement.
success_criteria.thin_clients: passed: no provider logic or provider-pi imports in clients; boundary test enforces this.
success_criteria.ghost_text: passed by static/host glue: VS Code returns InlineCompletionItem; Obsidian registers a CodeMirror ViewPlugin decoration and Tab keymap.

notes: No marketplace packaging or real-editor UI smoke was performed in this slice.
The Obsidian and VS Code host modules intentionally keep runtime-specific imports out of pure core modules so Node tests can cover request construction and cancellation without editor test harnesses.
Regenerate clients/protocol/src/generated with `cargo run -p autocomplete-protocol --bin export_client_artifacts -- clients/protocol/src/generated` when the Rust protocol changes.
queue_recommendation: Proceed to MVP-041 independent verification of editor clients, including actual VS Code/Obsidian smoke if available.
